raw_responses.query: LLM unlearning impossibility Loschmidt microreversibility machine unlearning 2025 arxiv

results.title: Unlearning Isn't Deletion: Investigating Reversibility of Machine Unlearning in LLMs
results.snippet: Models can appear to forget while their original behavior is easily restored through minimal fine-tuning. Four distinct forgetting regimes based on reversibility and catastrophicity. Irreversible non-catastrophic forgetting exceptionally challenging.

results.title: On the Impossibility of Retrain Equivalence in Machine Unlearning
results.snippet: Theory and experiments show multi-stage training introduces fundamental barrier for machine unlearning; local unlearning is path-dependent; impossible for path-oblivious algorithms to universally achieve Retrain Equivalence.

results.title: Machine Unlearning Meets Adversarial Robustness via Constrained Interventions on LLMs
results.snippet: Constrained interventions for unlearning under adversarial attack.

results.title: A Survey on Unlearning in Large Language Models
results.snippet: Survey of methods and challenges in LLM unlearning.

results.title: A Closer Look at Machine Unlearning for Large Language Models
results.snippet: Examines limitations of current unlearning methods.

results.title: A Survey of Machine Unlearning in Large Language Models: Methods, Challenges and Future Directions
LLM Unlearning Under the Microscope: A Full-Stack View on Methods and Metrics
results.snippet: Full-stack view of LLM unlearning methods and metrics.

raw_responses.query: "fundamental limit" OR "impossibility" LLM forgetting fine-tuning thermodynamic reversibility 2026

results.title: On the Fundamental Limits of LLMs at Scale
results.snippet: Proof-informed framework deriving hierarchy of impossibility and saturation results; triad: computational undecidability, statistical sample insufficiency, finite information capacity. No enumerable model class can be universally hallucination-free.

results.title: Mechanistic Analysis of Catastrophic Forgetting in LLMs During Continual Fine-tuning
results.snippet: Three mechanisms: gradient interference in attention weights, representational drift in intermediate layers, loss landscape flattening around prior task minima.

results.title: Unlearning Isn't Deletion (HTML)
results.snippet: Reversible (catastrophic) forgetting vs irreversible (catastrophic) forgetting. Feature subspaces preserved in reversible regime.

results.title: On the Fundamental Limits of LLMs at Scale (OpenReview)
